levelledPara.title: 1.1.1 The design of the BRAHMOS Fire Control System Mark-II (BFCS Mk-II) is modular in nature to reduce the MTTR. Various operations have been realized by adopting combined software - hardware approach. Standard reliable computer board and other PCBs / modules have been used to enhance the MTBF. There are hardly any components / parts that need regular maintenance. However, it is recommended that the user should carry out maintenance routines described in the succeeding paragraphs to keep the system operational.
1.1.2 Following precautions / protective measures are to be followed while handling, operating, and performing maintenance operation on the BFCS Mk-II:

note.notePara: (a) Ensure power to the unit is switched OFF while inserting or removing cards / modules.
(b) Ensure that all the cards / modules are inserted in the correct slots.
(c) Ensure that components mounted on cards / modules should not be touched with bare hand. Always wear anti-static wrist band while performing operations on these cards / modules.
(d) Ensure that all the cards are packed in anti-static covers.
(e) Ensure that all spares packed properly and kept in a dry and controlled environment.

levelledPara.para: (f) Use appropriate tools while handling / maintenance.
(h) Use only spares supplied in On Board Spares (OBS).
(j) Do not connect or remove cables in power ON conditions.

note.notePara: (k) All connectors should be covered with corresponding dust caps to prevent damage to the pins, when left de-mated.
(l) Always maintain a break of 15 minutes if the system is to be powered ON again after it has been switched OFF. However, this action should not be done frequently.
(m) Keeping the system powered ON continuously for a period of more than 168 hours is not recommended.
(n) Exposing the cards in extreme humid conditions for long time is to be avoided.
(p) While transporting the cards / modules, the same are to be wrapped in bubble sheet over the anti-static cover and vertically placed in a hard container. cards are not to be stacked.

levelledPara.title: 1.2.1.1 The periodic maintenance routines to be carried out on BFCS are given in the table 1.1. The maintenance routines are to be performed as per the time period specified in the table.

entry.para: Visual inspection and external cleaning of cabinets
Replacement of batteries used in BBC

levelledPara.para: 1.2.2.1 Visual Inspection and External Cleaning of Cabinets. Carry out visual inspection and cleaning of the system connectors, subunits and surrounding to prevent accumulation of dust and carbon particles. During the process: -

note.notePara: (a) Check for proper connectivity of all connectors;
(b) Visually inspect the units for any physical damage(s);
(c) Ensure proper cleanliness of compartment(s);
(d) Clean the cabinet and the display (externally) using soft dry cloth.

levelledPara.para: 1.2.2.2 Battery Checks. Carry out following checks to maintain the battery always serviceable: -

note.notePara: (a) Check the percentage charge of the battery on the battery health monitor on the BBC panel;
(b) If the charge is less than 90% follow the battery charging procedure given in para 1.2.2.3. If the charge has gone below 60%, follow the battery maintenance procedure given in para 1.2.2.4.

levelledPara.para: 1.2.2.3 Battery Charging Procedure. Follow step by step procedure given below for charging the battery: -

note.notePara: (a) Ensure that 230 V AC, Hz 1Φ ship’s mains supply is switched ON from the ships main and available at SFU-3. This will be indicated by glowing of green lamp (bubble ‘1’ of Fig. 1.1) on SFU-3.
(b) Ensure the heater switch (bubble ‘3’ of Fig. 1.1) is in ON position. In this position the SFU-3 is designed to provide mains (230 V AC, 50 Hz 1Φ) to the BBC through HSDB.
(c) Ensure that incoming power supply is available to the BBC unit. This is indicated by the glowing of the green lamp (bubble ‘1’ of Fig. 1.2) on the BBC front panel.

(d) Now, switch the main incomer switch (bubble ‘3’ of Fig. 1.2) to OPEN position which gives access to MCB located inside the main incomer switch. Push the MCB to ON position and keep the power rotary switch (bubble ‘4’ of Fig. 1.2) in OFF position for charging of the batteries.

levelledPara.para: Fig. 1.2 - Battery Bank and Charger Unit
levelledPara.note:
(e) Keep this configuration for 2-3 hours and monitor the percentage of charge with the battery monitor (bubble ‘2’ of Fig. 1.2), present on the front panel of the BBC.
(f) When the battery is fully charged, the 230 V AC, 50 Hz, 1 Φ ship’s main supply may be switched OFF from the ships main, if not required for the anti-condensation heaters. Also ensure that the output power rotary switch (bubble ‘4’ of Fig. 1.2) at the BBC remains in OFF position to prevent battery from discharging.
levelledPara.para: 1.2.2.4 Battery Maintenance Procedure. Replace battery as per the procedure for ‘Replacement of Battery’ referred herein, if it has been used for more than 2 years. Otherwise follow the steps given below: -

note.notePara: (a) Ensure that the power is switched OFF from the mains switchboard / ACOS as well as from SFU-3.
(b) Disconnect the battery terminals and put insulation tape on the disconnected wire terminals. Remove the batteries from BBC.
(c) Check battery voltage, it should not be less than 12.5 V for each battery (four batteries are connected in series - parallel combination to provide 24 V at required rating).
(d) If terminal voltage is found less than 12.5 V then charge the batteries independently for 8-10 hrs. through external charging arrangements.
(e) Check the battery voltage for each battery again after charging. If voltage is still less than 12.5 V, replace the relevant battery with new one.
(f) When the terminal voltage is found OK, then re-install the batteries, clean the wire terminals and reconnect the wire to the batteries terminals.

levelledPara.para: 1.2.2.5 Functional Checks. Carry out the functional checks on the system as follows to ensure its serviceability.

note.notePara: (a) Switch ON BFCS as per the powering ON procedure of BFCS laid out in the ‘User Handbook’.
(b) Ensure that MINS (Sigma 40 through RLG-DDU) has been switched ON and its feed is available on the CCU.
(c) Ensure that all external interfaces viz. CMS, SSR, NTP and SWISS are powered ON depending on their availability.
(d) Carry out the system maintenance by logging in the system as a maintainer as given in Chapter-2 of this manual.
(e) In case of any error, use the fault diagnosis checks given in Chapter-4 of this manual to troubleshoot the system.
(f) Switch OFF the BFCS as follows: -.

listItem.para: Logout and shutdown from the CCU screen. Switch the main rotary switch at SFU-3 to OFF position.
Switch the output power rotary switch on the BBC to OFF. Switch OFF the normal and alternate supply from MSB.

note.notePara: (g) Ensure availability of 230 V AC, 50 Hz 1Φ supply for the heaters (if required). Monitor the percentage charge on the battery. If it is below 90%, charge the battery as per procedure given in para 1.2.2.3.

levelledPara.para: 1.2.2.6 Battery Keep Alive Maintenance Procedure. Follow the steps given below to keep alive the batteries: -
levelledPara.note:
(a) Check the percentage of charge available on the battery. If it is below 90%, charge the batteries as per the procedure given in the para 1.2.2.3.
(b) Ensure that the power rotary switch on the BBC front panel is ON.
(c) Switch ON CCU by pressing the system ON IPB.
(d) Operate BFCS on battery power for 25-30 minutes.
(e) Check the percentage charge of the batteries.
(f) Charge the batteries again as per the procedure given in the para 1.2.2.3.

levelledPara.title: 1.2.2.7 Insulation Checks. Insulation checks of cables, as per the following procedure, are to be carried once in three months: -
levelledPara.note:
(a) Carry out insulation checks of power supply and analog signal cables running from MSU(s) to umbilical connector through LJB(s), using 100 V Megger after de-mating connectors J6, J7 and J8 at MSU(s) end. The insulation resistance of the cable(s) should be more than 1MΩ. Missile should not be there in the cell(s) during the checks.
(b) Carry out insulation checks of power cables running from ACOS> SFU-3>TRs / UVLM SFU using 1000 V megger after switching OFF MCCBs / switches on SFU-3 and ACOS. The insulation resistance of these power cables should be more than 10 MΩ. Use 500 V megger for insulation checks of cables carrying 230 V AC, 50 Hz 1Φ and 100 V megger for all other power cables and the insulation resistance should be more than 1 MΩ.

levelledPara.title: 1.2.2.8 Performance Checks. Performance checks as given below are to be carried once in three months: -
levelledPara.note:
(a) Perform checks as given in Test Nos. 1, 3, 4, 18, 6, 10 and 11 in Chapter-6 of Technical Manual Part-III ‘Overhaul and Reconditioning Instructions’.
(b) In case of any error, use the fault diagnosis checks given in Chapter-4 of this document to troubleshoot the system.

levelledPara.title: 1.2.2.9 Performance checks with TEM. The system performance is to be checked with Technology Missile once in every six months as given below: -

note.notePara: (a) Perform the checks given in Test No. 7 in Chapter-6 of the Technical Manual Part-III ‘Overhaul and Reconditioning Instructions’.
(b) In case of any error, use the fault diagnosis checks given in Chapter-4 of this document to troubleshoot the system.

levelledPara.para:
Note:
In case for any reason, performance checks cannot be carried out with the TEM, the same may be carried out using MLS and MES as per Test No. 5A in Chapter-6 of Technical Manual Part-III ‘Overhaul and Reconditioning Instructions’.

1.2.2.10 Replacement of Battery. Perform the operation as per the Technical Manual Part-III ‘Overhaul and Reconditioning Instructions’ for replacing the battery once every two years or whenever it goes defective.
HATS and SATS are not yet prepared by L&T, highlighted test nos. may get changed in approved HATS and SATS
